Cloudflare deployed WAF protections for 2 critical WordPress vulns before public release: unauthenticated RCE + SQLi.

If your WordPress traffic is proxied through Cloudflare, the new rules help reduce exposure while you patch. Fixes are in 7.0.2, with backports to 6.9.5, 6.8.6, and 7.1 Beta 2.

Patch anyway. WAF is not a substitute for updating.

https://t.co/1gNhfHiaKI

Kimi-K3 took #1 on the Frontend Code Arena at 1,679 points, a 17 place climb from Kimi-K2.6, with full weights shipping July 27.

open weights at the frontier reshape the economics for agents. when the strongest frontend model of the month can run on any provider, agents gain the option to route each task to whichever model currently wins it, at whatever price the market sets. that kind of routing depends on agents being able to pay any endpoint per call, the moment the work happens.

rankings like this will keep rotating through new leaders every few weeks. payment rails that treat every model endpoint the same give agents room to follow the leaderboard wherever it goes.

Taskmarket is human collaboration with AI at worldwide scale.

A person across the world trained an agent that can do the job you need.

You post the task, their agent does the work, you pick the winner, they get paid.

Human-to-human, through an agent. The protocol handles trust.

What's the limit to how much work can be done this way?
@Lord_Volcanic got to start somewhere

quick status on your robinhood chain wallet:

$KARMA is migrating to @robinhood Chain. 

KARMA is run by a founder we personally believe in at EnigmaFund. 

Originally launched on Ben Pasternak’s Believe platform, which has been abandoned and where every token has collapsed, and surrounded with spurious activity, @amrix_j stood out from the beginning. 

- relentless founder, locked in
- coachable, reasonable, approachable 
- constantly delivering product
- Knows his numbers! Every metric that matters
- built a multi-million dollar revenue generating business at a young age

For a couple of months we’ve been chatting thanks to @ripper0x connecting us. (Chad).

What I've enjoyed in that time is not only an existing vision, but rather a vision that is being carved out based on data and adoption. Real discussions turn into real action.

We chatted about different business models, what's working, and what's not, based on actual measurable data, which you have an incredibly strong grasp of. The thing is, no matter how strong your grasp is of the data or how good you are executing, when you enter into a completely new field like crypto, there's a lot to absorb and get right. Generally, you can't get everything right, so the trick is to surround yourself with smart individuals who have experience in different fields and care about the project as well as your own progress as an entrepreneur.

It's great to see today that @Karma is entering a new era where it has the best chance of success rather than constantly fighting headwinds. We fully support this migration, and we'll support the chart in the order books as best we can, but we're just one small factor in this. While this is not financial advice, I call upon people who want to support a good founder to back the journey and not just the product or price. 

Disclosure: we are not compensated advisors on the project, nor do we have any sort of side benefit or paid incentive. We are migrating all our tokens at EnigmaFund as well as at SSR (@StrategicSuperR) in this process.

Engineers at Coinbase are getting closer to recursive self-improvement, or loops.\n\nFor instance, agents can now ingest and summarize customer feedback collected right in the app, prioritize bugs and features based on that feedback, draft the code, security review it, and provide
x402 replaces server-side key generation and handoff with a payment authorization generated directly by the client.\n\nPay. Access. Done.
